01_initial_request.input_data: cryo-EM density maps and amino acid contact predictions from sequence coevolutionary information
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop a method for de novo building of atomic models from near-atomic resolution cryo-EM density maps where side-chain densities are poorly resolved
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Generation of an atomic model with correctly segmented subunits and assigned amino acid sequences to backbone traces

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The cryo-EM density map of the asymmetric unit is available as input (EMD-2513, 80×74×76 grid points, 1.32Å spacing). [Gap] The map needs to be converted into a graph representation to enable backbone tracing. [Decision] To achieve graph-based backbone tracing, chose to use custom graph algorithm to perform EM map to weighted graph conversion on the cryo-EM density map, expecting to obtain a weighted graph G(V,E) where vertices are grid points and edges connect 26 direct neighbors.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Weighted graph G(V,E) created where vertices are grid points and edge weights are inverse of product of corresponding densities

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The EM density map has been converted to a weighted graph. [Gap] The graph needs to be simplified to find the optimal backbone threading path. [Decision] To achieve backbone path identification, chose to use Kruskal algorithm to perform minimum spanning tree construction on the weighted graph, expecting to obtain a minimum spanning tree that encompasses high-density regions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Minimum spanning tree (MSTree) derived from the EM density map graph

references.relevance: Provides the algorithm foundation for minimum spanning tree construction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The minimum spanning tree has been constructed from the EM density graph. [Gap] The tree contains vertices with degree higher than 3, which doesn't match Cα atom bonding patterns. [Decision] To achieve Cα trace candidate identification, chose to use custom pruning algorithm to perform tree pruning on the MSTree, expecting to obtain a pruned tree with maximal degree of 3.

parameters.rationale: Cα atoms are covalently bonded to three non-hydrogen atoms at most

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Tree pruned until maximal degree is 3, providing potential candidate for Cα trace threading

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The tree has been pruned to maximal degree 3. [Gap] The vertex spacing (1.32Å) doesn't match the average Cα-Cα distance. [Decision] To achieve accurate Cα atom placement, chose to use custom refinement algorithm to perform Cα position refinement on the pruned tree vertices, expecting to obtain vertices separated by approximately 3.8Å.

parameters.anchoring_points: nodes of degree 3 (likely Cα atoms with visible side-chain density)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Vertex coordinates refined to get as many vertices separated by 3.8Å as possible

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cα positions have been refined to 3.8Å spacing. [Gap] The resulting graph still contains forks that need to be removed to get unbranched fragments. [Decision] To achieve unbranched backbone fragments, chose to use custom fork pruning procedure to perform fork removal on the refined graph, expecting to obtain unbranched fragments approximating backbone threading.

parameters.procedure: remove shortest path graph when fork detected if length < T_p, otherwise delete fork nodes

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Unbranched fragments generated that approximate the backbone threading in the EM density map

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Backbone fragments have been extracted from the EM density map. [Gap] Contact predictions are needed to guide fragment assembly and sequence assignment. [Decision] To achieve contact-based fragment assembly, chose to use RaptorX ContactMap server to perform contact prediction on the protein sequences, expecting to obtain predicted inter-residue contacts with probability scores.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 658, 343, and 563 predicted contacts for chains A, B, and C respectively (for F420-reducing hydrogenase test case)

references.citation: Wang, S. et al. 2017, PLoS Comput. Biol.
references.relevance: Describes the RaptorX contact prediction method used

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Backbone fragments and predicted contacts are available. [Gap] Fragments need to be assembled and oriented to match the predicted contact patterns. [Decision] To achieve fragment assembly with sequence assignment, chose to use map_align algorithm to perform contact map alignment on the fragments, expecting to obtain fragment concatenation that maximizes overlap with predicted contacts.

parameters.optimization: maximize overlapping contacts while minimizing gaps

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fragments concatenated with direction determined to maximize agreement with predicted contacts

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fragments have been assembled using contact map alignment. [Gap] Multiple chains need to be built sequentially to segment the EM map into individual subunits. [Decision] To achieve map segmentation and chain assignment, chose to use custom iterative concatenation algorithm to perform chain building on the fragment pool, expecting to obtain segmented chains with assigned sequences.

parameters.procedure: build chains sequentially, remove used fragments from pool after each chain
parameters.optimization: dynamic programming to maximize overlap with predicted contacts

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 18 fragments found with average accuracy of 1.2Å Cα RMSD; chains A, B, and C detected automatically without prior segmentation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cα traces with assigned sequences have been generated for all chains. [Gap] Full atomic models need to be constructed from the Cα traces. [Decision] To achieve full atomic model generation, chose to use Modeller to perform atomic model building on the Cα traces, expecting to obtain full-atom models of the protein structure.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Full-atom model of the structure obtained from Cα tracing

references.citation: Eswar, N. et al. 2006, Curr. Protoc. Bioinform.
references.relevance: Describes Modeller for comparative protein structure modeling

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Full atomic models have been built from Cα traces. [Gap] Models need to be refined against the EM density map for optimal fit. [Decision] To achieve model refinement against experimental data, chose to use Phenix real_space_refine tool to perform model refinement on the atomic models, expecting to obtain refined models optimized against the EM map.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Model refined in the EM map with secondary structures optimized

references.citation: Afonine, P.V. et al. 2013, Comput. Crystallogr. Newsl.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Structural alignment and comparison with reference structure (PDB 4CI0) using Cα RMSD, contact map overlap score, and Segment Manders' Overlap Coefficient (SMOC)

fragment_accuracy.interpretation: Average Cα RMSD of generated fragments to reference structure

chain_A_deviation.interpretation: Average Cα deviation for chain A from reference structure

chain_B_deviation.interpretation: Average Cα deviation for chain B from reference structure (less accurate due to sparse contacts and low local resolution)

chain_C_deviation.interpretation: Average Cα deviation for chain C from reference structure

overall_accuracy.interpretation: Average Cα RMSD to deposited structure across all chains

fragment_coverage.interpretation: Generated fragments fully cover the three chains of the reference structure

T6SS_TssF_confirmation.interpretation: Cα RMSD for TssF confirmed by later higher resolution cryo-EM maps

T6SS_TssG_confirmation.interpretation: Cα RMSD for TssG confirmed by later higher resolution cryo-EM maps

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The method successfully generated atomic models with correctly segmented subunits and assigned amino acid sequences to backbone traces from near-atomic resolution cryo-EM density maps (3.36Å for F420-reducing hydrogenase). The approach correctly segmented the EM map into individual subunits without prior segmentation and assigned sequences using contact predictions from coevolutionary information. Average accuracy of 2.4Å Cα RMSD demonstrates the method's effectiveness, though performance varies with contact prediction quality and local map resolution.
